title: Effects of Non-Luminous Planets
structure: 7 upagrahas x 12 houses = 84 placements + 1 intro + 2 concluding principles

claims.claim: Introduction: results due to Dhuma/Gulika etc. mature in Dasa periods of their dispositors
Dhuma in 1st → valiant, beautiful eyes, stupefied, unkind, wicked, short-tempered
Dhuma in 2nd → sickly, wealthy, royal humiliation, dullwitted, eunuch
Dhuma in 3rd → intelligent, bold, delighted, eloquent, wealthy
Dhuma in 4th → grieved by female giving up, learned in all sastras
Dhuma in 5th → limited progeny, devoid of wealth, eat anything, bereft of friends/mantras
Dhuma in 6th → strong, conquer enemies, brilliant, famous, free from diseases
Dhuma in 7th → penniless, sensuous, others' females, devoid of brilliance
Dhuma in 8th → bereft of courage, enthusiastic, truthful, disagreeable, hard-hearted, selfish
Dhuma in 9th → sons, fortunes, rich, honourable, kind, religious, well-disposed to relatives
Dhuma in 10th → sons, fortunes, delighted, intelligent, happy, truthful
Dhuma in 11th → wealth, grains, gold, beautiful, knowledge of arts, modest, skilful singing
Dhuma in 12th → morally fallen, sinful acts, others' wives, addicted to vices, unkind, crafty
Vyatipata in 1st → troubled by miseries, cruel, destructive acts, foolish, ill-disposed to relatives
Vyatipata in 2nd → morally crooked, bilious, enjoy pleasures, unkind but grateful, wicked, sinful
Vyatipata in 3rd → firm disposition, warrior, liberal, very rich, dear to king, head of army
Vyatipata in 4th → endowed with relatives but not sons and fortunes
Vyatipata in 5th → poor, charming, imbalances phlegm/bile/wind, hard-hearted, shameless
Vyatipata in 6th → destroy enemies, physically mighty, skilful weapons/arts, peaceful
Vyatipata in 7th → bereft wealth/wife/sons, subdue to females, miserable, sensuous, shameless
Vyatipata in 8th → deformity eyes, ugly, unfortunate, spiteful to Brahmins, blood disorders
Vyatipata in 9th → many business/friends, very learned, well-disposed wife, eloquent
Vyatipata in 10th → rich, religious, peaceful, skilful religious acts, learned, farsighted
Vyatipata in 11th → extremely opulent, honourable, truthful, firm policy, many horses, singing
Vyatipata in 12th → anger, many activities, disabled, irreligious, hate own relatives
Paridhi in 1st → learned, truthful, peaceful, rich, sons, pure, charitable, dear to elders
Paridhi in 2nd → wealthy, charming, happy, very religious, be a lord
Paridhi in 3rd → fond of wife, charming, pious, well-disposed to men, servant, respectful elders
Paridhi in 4th → wonderstruck, helpful to enemies, kind, endowed with everything, skilful singing
Paridhi in 5th → affluent, virtuous, splendorous, affectionate, religious, dear to wife
Paridhi in 6th → famous, wealthy, sons, pleasures, helpful to all, conquer enemies
Paridhi in 7th → limited children, devoid happiness, mediocre intelligence, hard-hearted, sickly wife
Paridhi in 8th → spiritually disposed, peaceful, strong-bodied, firm decision, religious, gentle
Paridhi in 9th → happy, brilliant, affluent, sons, devoid excessive passion, honourable
Paridhi in 10th → versed in arts, enjoy pleasures, strong-bodied, learned in all sastras
Paridhi in 11th → pleasures through women, virtuous, intelligent, dear to people, digestive fire disorders
Paridhi in 12th → spendthrift, miserable, firm, dishonour elders
Chapa in 1st → wealth, grains, gold, grateful, agreeable, devoid all afflictions
Chapa in 2nd → speak affably, very rich, modest, learned, charming, religious
Chapa in 3rd → miser, versed in many arts, thieving, devoid of some limb, unfriendly
Chapa in 4th → happy, quadrupeds/wealth/grains, honoured by king, devoid sickness
Chapa in 5th → splendorous, farsighted, pious, affable, prosperity all undertakings
Chapa in 6th → destroy enemies, happy, affectionate, pure, plentifulness all undertakings
Chapa in 7th → wealthy, all virtues, learned in sastras, religious, agreeable
Chapa in 8th → interested in others' jobs, cruel, others' wives, defective limbed
Chapa in 9th → penance, religious observations, highly learned, famous
Chapa in 10th → many sons, abundant wealth, cows/buffaloes, famous
Chapa in 11th → gainful, free from diseases, fiery disposition, affectionate wife, mantras/weapons
Chapa in 12th → wicked, very honourable, evil disposition, shameless, others' females, poor
Dhwaja in 1st → skilful all learning, happy, efficient speech, agreeable, affectionate
Dhwaja in 2nd → good speaker, splendorous, write poetry, scholarly, honourable, modest, conveyances
Dhwaja in 3rd → miserly, cruel in acts, thin-bodied, poor, severe diseases
Dhwaja in 4th → charming, very virtuous, gentle, interested in Vedas, always happy
Dhwaja in 5th → happy, enjoy pleasures, versed in arts, skilled expedients, intelligent, eloquent, respect elders
Dhwaja in 6th → ominous for maternal relatives, win enemies, many relatives, valiant, splendorous, skilful
Dhwaja in 7th → interested in gambling, sensuous, enjoy pleasures, befriend prostitutes
Dhwaja in 8th → interested in base acts, sinful, shameless, blame others, lack marital happiness, take others' side
Dhwaja in 9th → wear religious badges, delighted, helpful to all, skilled religious deeds
Dhwaja in 10th → happiness, fortunes, fond of females, charitable, befriend Brahmins
Dhwaja in 11th → ever acquire gains, very religious, honourable, affluent, fortunate, valiant, sacrificial rites
Dhwaja in 12th → sinful acts, valiant, untrustworthy, unkind, others' females, short-tempered
Gulika in 1st → afflicted by diseases, lustful, sinful, crafty, wicked, miserable
Notes: severe defects of eyes, thieving, disregard religion, deprived progeny, longevity cut, dullheaded
Gulika in 2nd → unsightly, miserable, given to vices, shameless, penniless
Notes: learning obstructed, speech defects, harsh speech, not have family happiness, untruthful, scandal, death-like troubles in 2nd lord dasa
Gulika in 3rd → charming, head a village, fond of virtuous men, honoured by king
Notes: destruction of co-born, though fairly rich will feel distressed
Gulika in 4th → sickly, devoid of happiness, sinful, afflicted by windy/bilious excesses
Notes: inimical to all including relatives, devoid of conveyances, risks through conveyances
Gulika in 5th → not praiseworthy, poor, short-lived, spiteful, mean, eunuch, subdued by wife, heterodox
Notes: virility/progenic ability significantly affected, not obtain issues unless Jupiter and 5th lord favourable
Gulika in 6th → devoid of enemies, strong-bodied, spleenorous, liked by wife, enthusiastic, helpful
Notes: control evil spirits, obtain children, very courageous, freedom from diseases if benefic sign
Gulika in 7th → subdue to spouse, sinful, others' females, emaciated, devoid friendship, live on wife's wealth
Notes: thrive on female's wealth, conjugal life no happiness, possibly more than one marriage
Gulika in 8th → troubled by hunger, miserable, cruel, short-tempered, unkind, poor, bereft good qualities
Notes: difficult to get square meal, ugly face, diseased eyes, yellowish teeth, short stature
Gulika in 9th → undergo ordeals, emaciated, evil acts, very unkind, sluggish, talebearer
Notes: devoid paternal bliss, father passes in childhood, none of undertakings bear fruits
Gulika in 10th → endowed with sons, happy, enjoy many things, fond worship gods/fire, meditation/religion
Notes: favourable for Yoga/Meditation, but at one stage turn heterodox and discard religious code
Gulika in 11th → enjoy women of class, leader of men, helpful to relatives, short, emperor
Notes: company of many females, devoid of good character, progenic happiness, wealth, charming
Gulika in 12th → indulge in base deeds, sinful, defective-limbed, unfortunate, indolent, join mean people
Notes: innumerable misfortunes, loss of wealth on evil missions, but enjoy progenic happiness
Pranapada in 1st → weak, sickly, dullwitted, defective-limbed, miserable, emaciated
Pranapada in 2nd → abundant grains, wealth, children, attendants, fortunate
Pranapada in 3rd → injurious/mischievous, proud, hard-hearted, dirty, no respect for elders
Pranapada in 4th → happy, attached to females/elders, soft, truthful
Pranapada in 5th → happy, do good acts, kind, very affectionate
Pranapada in 6th → subdued by relatives/enemies, sharp, defective digestive fire, wicked, sickly, affluent, shortlived
Pranapada in 7th → green-eyed, ever libidinous, fierce appearance, not worth respect, ill-disposed
Pranapada in 8th → afflicted by diseases, troubled, incur misery from king/relatives/servants/sons
Pranapada in 9th → endowed with sons, very rich, fortunate, charming, serve others, not wicked, skilful
Pranapada in 10th → heroic, intelligent, skilful, expert in royal orders, worship gods
Pranapada in 11th → famous, virtuous, learned, wealthy, fair-complexioned, attached to mother
Pranapada in 12th → mean, wicked, defective-limbed, hate Brahmins/relatives, eye diseases, one-eyed
PRINCIPLE: Before declaring results of Dhooma/Gulika etc., consider Sun and other planets' strength, relations, aspects

PRINCIPLE: Check dispositors — if Gulika gives bad effects but dispositor is well-placed/aspected, evils are minimised. Conversely if favourable effects with weak dispositor, good effects don't mature
PRINCIPLE: Balance results of Dhooma etc. with results of planets from Sun to Saturn (and nodes)
